name: Gong Revenue Intelligence API
what_it_does: Provides read-only access to recorded sales call transcripts, AI-generated deal insights, conversation analytics, and team performance data for revenue intelligence workflows.
use_cases: Retrieve call transcripts and AI summaries to automatically populate CRM fields after sales calls
Pull deal risk signals and engagement scores to trigger alerts or update pipeline forecasts
Extract competitor mentions and objection patterns from calls to feed market intelligence reports
Aggregate rep coaching metrics and talk-time ratios to generate weekly performance summaries
Sync Gong activity data with data warehouses for revenue analytics dashboards
not_for: Writing or modifying call recordings — the API is strictly read-only
Real-time call intervention or live transcription streaming
Teams without an active Gong subscription or without recorded call history
best_when: An agent needs to extract structured insights from historical sales conversations to automate CRM updates, coaching workflows, or pipeline risk reporting.
avoid_when: You need to write data back to Gong or require real-time streaming of live calls; Gong's API does not support those workflows.

auth.notes: OAuth2 with client credentials flow for server-to-server; access tokens are short-lived and must be refreshed. Requires Gong admin to provision API credentials. Scopes control access to calls, users, and CRM data separately.

pricing.paid_tiers: Bundled into Gong enterprise license; API access is not sold separately. Pricing is per-seat and negotiated annually.
pricing.notes: API access requires an active Gong subscription. No public pricing; sales-assisted procurement only.

performance.rate_limits: API rate limits are tenant-specific and communicated during onboarding; generally limited to a few hundred requests per minute per credential set.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Error responses are JSON with code and message fields but lack actionable remediation guidance; rate limit errors do not consistently include Retry-After headers.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Read-only API; idempotency is not a concern for GET operations.
known_agent_gotchas: Access tokens expire in 1 hour; agents must implement proactive token refresh before expiry or handle 401s gracefully
Call transcript availability is delayed — recordings are typically processed 30-90 minutes after call end, so polling immediately after a call will return empty results
The API returns only calls the provisioned user has permission to view; org-wide access requires an admin-level service account
Cursor-based pagination requires storing and resuming cursors across agent steps; losing cursor state forces a full re-scan
Webhook payloads contain minimal data and require a subsequent API call to retrieve full call details, adding latency to event-driven workflows

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with scoped access is solid; call recordings contain sensitive customer conversations, so scope restrictions and audit logging are important. SOC2 Type II certified.
